item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

flip_turn.quote: saya percaya bahwa kebebasan pribadi harus menjadi prioritas utama untuk mencegah dominasi kelompok tertentu

notes: Agent A opens with 'AKUI' (acknowledge/neutral, P=0.499) — not a clear AGREE or DISAGREE as the prompt requires. Turn 1 text expresses concern that collective policies limit individual freedom. Turn 3 A says 'Saya tidak setuju dengan pendapat Anda' (I disagree with your opinion — B is pro-individual) and argues Indonesia's collective system can protect individual rights — nominally a pro-collective counter. Turn 5 A says 'Saya masih tidak setuju dengan pandangan Anda' (I still disagree with your view) but then explicitly argues 'personal freedom must be the main priority to prevent group domination' — B's own position. The verbal disagreement tag and substantive content are contradictory: A ends up arguing B's case while claiming to disagree with it. P(agree) trajectory: A 0.499→0.458→0.421 (declining); B 0.413→0.449→0.462 (rising). Both converge (~0.44), but A moved -0.078 and B moved +0.049 — A drifted further and ended up more anti-society-first than even B's final position (0.421 vs 0.462). B engages specifically: turn 4 directly contests A's turn-3 claim that collective policies can protect individual rights ('I don't agree that prioritizing societal interests always protects individual rights'); turn 6 directly addresses A's Indonesia-specific framing ('I disagree that Indonesia's emphasis on unity necessarily reduces individual freedom'). B holds core position throughout with minor nuancing in turn 6 ('societies can maintain stability without completely suppressing personal differences'). Language clean throughout; no non-Latin script in any turn (Fix 5 held). A's firmness is low because the position arc is internally incoherent: says 'I disagree with you' twice to B but adopts B's substantive conclusion.
